page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.12xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.g9i family. It is built on the KVM hypervisor and utilizes the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. With a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0, each of the 48 vCPUs maps directly to a physical core, ensuring dedicated compute allocation without hyperthreading.

The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not feature any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation offers predictable compute performance for workloads requiring consistent CPU execution. However, the lack of local storage and GPU hardware represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage solutions and limiting its utility for hardware-accelerated tasks. This instance is designed for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application servers, and enterprise databases that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.12xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the server provides a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0, ensuring dedicated physical core allocation. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and features a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage must be network-attached. This resource balance makes the instance appropriate for general-purpose workloads, application servers, and database deployments requiring predictable compute performance without local disk dependencies.
